setup.label: ConsolePluginSetup
description: Console plugin's setup service object

children.label: locator
description: Public locator for the console UI

start.label: ConsolePluginStart
description: Console plugin's start service object

children.label: isEmbeddedConsoleAvailable
description:
isEmbeddedConsoleAvailable is available if the embedded console can be rendered. Returns true when
called if the Embedded Console is currently rendered.

children.label: openEmbeddedConsole
description:
openEmbeddedConsole is available if the embedded console can be rendered. Calling
this function will open the embedded console on the page if it is currently rendered.

children.label: openEmbeddedConsoleAlternateView
description:
openEmbeddedConsoleAlternateView is available if the embedded console can be rendered.
Calling this function will open the embedded console to the alternative view. If there is no alternative view registered
this will open the embedded console.

children.label: EmbeddableConsole
description: EmbeddableConsole is a functional component used to render a portable version of the dev tools console on any page in Kibana

children.label: registerEmbeddedConsoleAlternateView
description:
Register an alternate view for the Embedded Console

When registering an alternate view ensure that the content component you register is lazy loaded.

children.label: methods
description: HTTP request methods this endpoint accepts: GET, POST, DELETE etc

children.label: patterns
description:
URLs paths this endpoint accepts, can contain parameters in curly braces.
For example, /_cat/indices/{index}

children.label: url_components
description: List of possible values for parameters used in patterns.

children.label: url_params
description: Query parameters for this endpoint.

children.label: data_autocomplete_rules
description: Request body parameters for this endpoint.

children.label: priority
description: A priority number when the same endpoint name is used.

children.label: documentation
description:
An url of the documentation page of this endpoint.
Can contain a parameter {branch}.

children.label: documentation_serverless
description:
Serverless url of the documentation page of this endpoint.
When `endpointsAvailability` is `serverless`, Console may use this value
(if present) instead of the generic API reference link.

children.label: availability
description: If the endpoint is available different environments (stack, serverless).

children.label: create
description: Creates a new object in the underlying persistance layer.

children.label: update
description: This method should update specific object in the persistance layer.

children.label: findAll
description: A function that will return all of the objects in the persistance layer.

interfaces.label: TextObject
description:
Describes the shape of persisted objects that contain information about the current text in the
text editor.

children.label: id
description: An ID that uniquely identifies this object.

children.label: createdAt
description: UNIX timestamp of when the object was created.

children.label: updatedAt
description: UNIX timestamp of when the object was last updated.

children.label: text
description:
Text value input by the user.

Used to re-populate a text editor buffer.

misc.label: DEFAULT_INPUT_VALUE
signature:
"# Welcome to the Dev Tools Console!
#
# You can use Console to explore the Elasticsearch API. See the Elasticsearch API reference to learn more:
# https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/rest-apis.html
#
# Here are a few examples to get you started.


# Create an index
PUT /my-index


# Add a document to my-index
POST /my-index/_doc
{
    "id": "park_rocky-mountain",
    "title": "Rocky Mountain",
    "description": "Bisected north to south by the Continental Divide, this portion of the Rockies has ecosystems varying from over 150 riparian lakes to montane and subalpine forests to treeless alpine tundra."
}


# Perform a search in my-index
GET /my-index/_search?q="rocky mountain""

misc.label: DEFAULT_VARIABLES
signature: { id: string; name: string; value: string; }[]
